calendar.matters:
Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion For Clarification Of Order Signed On June 21 2016 And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Sep-28-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeffrey Frasier (via Court Call) for Linda Gaetke. Paul Melbostad for (trustee) Debra Tyler. Denise Depropris (assistant to Mr. Melbostad) also present.

Mr. Melbostad presented oral argument stating that we need to focus on the period after the 12/15/15 notice of entry of order, that it says Ms. Tyler produced nothing before or after and ignored Court orders, counsel read from emails regarding documents being requested and provided, that on 1/22/16 they confirmed to Mr. Frasier what had been provided and requested that they not request additional documents as they are ready for distribution, that they did not receive a response until 2/2/16 stating they were having difficulty putting everything together and that orders had not been complied with. Mr. Melbostad further stated that they keep asking for more statements and cancelled checks, that they are trying to extort money from Ms. Tyler and that they are requesting more money than is in the trust. Ms. Depropris reported that she took over the document production for this case in March of 2016, that she repeatedly asked what else was needed, that they did not receive a response until 5/3/16 as to what was missing, Ms. Gaetke then provided account numbers for account statements not produced, that they have had trouble obtaining older statements from Wells Fargo Bank and that it took many emails to Wells Fargo to get everything needed. Mr. Melbostad pointed out that Ms. Gaetke was the trustee until her father died and had personal knowledge of account numbers, that Article 15 of the trust has a difficult beneficiary clause and unless a trustee is guilty of gross negligence he/she is not required to pay out of his/her personal funds. Mr. Frasier stated that he disagrees with everything, that this is not a motion to rehash the issues, that the ruling is correct, that they are here to clarify some confusion in the order as to where the funds for the attorney's fees would come from, that they could not start the process until all of the information was received, that if the trust pays the award of fees then Ms. Gaetke would be paying 50% out of her money, that the new accounts were found in the information provided by Ms. Tyler and they then asked for the information, that Ms. Tyler had the account numbers all along, that this is the first they are hearing of the difficult beneficiary clause and cannot address it today and that the fees should be paid by Ms. Tyler out of her money. Mr. Melbostad stated that the order says that the trustee is to pay the attorney's fees, that they need to reach an understanding of where this is going, that while Inez was alive Ms. Tyler's fiduciary duty was to Inez only, that they are asking for statements and cancelled checks back to 2008 when the bankers have made it clear that they could only go back to 2011, that they have a $35,000.00 check ready to send to Linda but if they keep requesting copies of documents that will diminish and that within 2 weeks of selling the house Linda was given a check for $300,000.00.
The Court requested that the parties submit their respective proposed orders no later than September 26, 2016 and the Motion for Clarification of Order Signed on June 21, 2016 will be under submission as of that date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar May-04-2016 Continued To May-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To:1.Compel Accounting From Trustee; 2.Instruct The Trustee To Wind Up And Close Trust;And
Off Calendar Jun-29-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeffrey Frasier (via court call) for Linda Gaetke. Paul Melbostad for (trustee) Debra Tyler.

Mr. Frasier reported that he filed a declaration last week, that nothing has changed, that he would like a ruling on the petition, that he would like the information ordered by the court, that Ms. Tyler is not performing her duties, that she is serving without a bond, that she is taking a trustee payment each year and that the trust needs to be closed out. Mr. Melbostad stated that he should have filed an opposition, that he was hoping to resolve this informally, that Ms. Taylor arranged for 24 hour care for her mother, that Ms. Taylor's only fiduciary duty was to her mother, that she deferred payment to herself while her mother was still alive and that all of the money was applied to paying for in home care and that they have not provided records for the time period that Inez was alive. Mr. Frasier stated that nothing has been provided since the 4/24/15 court order, that they have received nothing relating to subsections A, C & D, that they have received some bank information relating to subsection B, that in regards to subsection A they have received some reports on withdrawal but no checks or other documentation. Mr. Melbostad stated that Mr. Frasier is correct and that he will file a motion to vacate the order as Inez was alive during that time period. Mr. Melbostad further stated that they have filed the final tax return, that they are ready for final distribution, that litigation will deplete the funds and that he will ask the trustee for invoices. The court asked Mr. Melbostad about venue. Mr. Melbostad stated that the filed document stated that administration of the trust was in San Francisco. Mr. Frasier cited 13.2 that an annual accounting is required to the settlor and that if this has been done it will be easy to produce. Mr. Melbostad responded that the residuary had to ask for the accounting while the lifetime beneficiary was alive. The petition to compel an accounting from the trustee, etc., was taken under submission. Mr. Frasier is to submit a revised order.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition To:1.Compel Accounting From Trustee; 2.Instruct The Trustee To Wind Up And Close Trust;And
Off Calendar May-18-2015 Continued To Jun-29-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To:1.Compel Accounting From Trustee; 2.Instruct The Trustee To Wind Up And Close Trust;And
Off Calendar Feb-23-2015 Continued To May-18-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeffrey Frasier for (petitioner) Linda Gaetke via court call. Paul Melbostad for (trustee) Debra Tyler.

Mr. Melbostad stated that they are willing to provide the requested information and that the trustee has hired a CPA. Mr. Frasier stated that a formal accounting is not needed if the missing items (bank and mortgage statements) are provided, that they have been asking for this since last May and that deadlines are needed. Mr. Melbostad stated that he needs 60-90 days, will call Mr. Frasier to go over the list of requested documents and that the reverse mortgage statements have been ordered. The court requested that Mr. Frasier submit a proposed order regarding a deadline of May 4, 2015 to produce documents and continued the petition to compel accounting from trustee, etc. to May 18, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Murillo. (Not Reported)
